title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. heterophyllus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. heterophyllus, also known as water buttercup, is a subspecies of aquatic plant native to North America, characterized by its distinctive leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat wounds, reduce pain, and alleviate fever. The health benefits of water buttercup include its potential to reduce swelling, promote healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. Water buttercup is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
